product.name: OMRON Evolv Wireless Upper Arm Blood Pressure Monitor

pilotSummary.bestFor: People who want a compact one-piece upper-arm monitor with an attached cuff, optional app syncing, and documented 22 to 42 cm arm fit
pilotSummary.verdict: The OMRON Evolv BP7000 is a well-documented one-piece upper-arm monitor with FDA clearance, a current official US product page, a five-year warranty, and model-family validation evidence. It is not a true fixed-opening arm-in monitor, and the STRIDE BP listing uses HEM-7600T while the US manual pairs BP7000 with HEM-7600T-Z, so the suffix mapping remains a material caveat.

notes: FDA K162092 identifies the US trade/device name as Evolv Model BP7000 Upper Arm Blood Pressure Monitor.
The official US manual lists BP7000 and HEM-7600T-Z together in its specifications table.
STRIDE BP lists HEM-7600T without the Z suffix. Treat the validation listing as strong model-family evidence, not a fully resolved suffix-level identity match.
The official US product page displayed MSRP, specifications, warranty, and retailer links on 2026-07-21.

Exact BP7000 and OMRON Evolv openFDA recall/enforcement queries returned no matches on 2026-07-21. No-match queries do not prove that no recall, correction, alternate-name record, or non-US action exists.

facts.normalizedFact: FDA 510(k) K162092 identifies the Evolv Model BP7000 Upper Arm Blood Pressure Monitor as a Class II noninvasive blood pressure measurement system under product code DXN.
The official OMRON US instruction manual lists BP7000 and HEM-7600T-Z in the same specifications table; STRIDE BP separately lists HEM-7600T, without the Z suffix.

OMRON describes the Evolv BP7000 as a one-piece upper-arm monitor with an attached ComFit cuff and no separate hoses or desk display; it still wraps around the arm and is not a fixed arm-opening device.
The official manual specifies an arm-circumference range of 22 to 42 cm, which the US product page presents as approximately 9 to 17 inches.
The FDA 510(k) summary states that the BP7000 cuff is not replaceable.

The official manual states a pressure accuracy specification of plus or minus 3 mmHg and says the monitor was clinically investigated according to ISO 81060-2:2013; FDA K162092 includes nonclinical and clinical testing summaries for BP7000.
STRIDE BP lists OMRON HEM-7600T as an available preferred home upper-arm device for adults, with validated cuff sizes of 22 to 42 cm and an ANSI/AAMI/ISO 2013 validation study published in 2021.

The manual states that the monitor can store up to 100 measurements internally, but prior stored readings cannot be viewed on the monitor without transferring them to a smart device; OMRON says current measurements can be taken without a smart device.

The official US page displayed a $119.99 MSRP and a five-year limited warranty on 2026-07-21; the manual states that the five-year warranty applies to the original retail purchaser when bought from an authorized OMRON seller, subject to its terms.

Exact BP7000 and OMRON Evolv openFDA device recall/enforcement queries returned no matching records on 2026-07-21; this limited no-match result is not proof that no recall, correction, differently named record, or non-US action exists.

limitations: MDRank did not perform hands-on or laboratory testing for this dossier.
The Evolv BP7000 is one-piece and tubeless, but it is not a true fixed-opening arm-in or barrel-style monitor.
The FDA summary states that the integrated cuff is not replaceable, which may matter for long-term ownership and damage outside warranty coverage.

The STRIDE BP listing is HEM-7600T while the current US manual lists HEM-7600T-Z alongside BP7000; the exact significance of the suffix requires confirmation before treating the registry entry as an exact US-package match.
Manufacturer ease-of-use, portability, movement-interference, and clinically accurate wording remains manufacturer-attributed unless independently supported.
The app is optional for taking a current reading, but the manual says a smart device is required to view prior stored readings.
HSA/FSA eligibility has not been verified for the exact current offer and should not be claimed.
MSRP, retailer availability, app behavior, and support terms can change and require refreshes.
